title: Poisoning (Ingestion/Inhalation)
immediate_action: Call Poison Control or emergency services. Identify the substance if possible.

steps.instruction: Call emergency services or Poison Control. Tell them: what was swallowed, how much, when, and the person's age/weight.
steps.tip: Keep the container/packaging — paramedics need to know the exact substance.

steps.instruction: Do NOT induce vomiting unless specifically told to by Poison Control. Many substances cause more damage coming back up.
steps.tip: Corrosives (bleach, drain cleaner) and petroleum products MUST NOT be vomited.

steps.instruction: If the person is conscious and alert, and Poison Control advises: they may recommend drinking water or milk to dilute.
steps.tip: Follow Poison Control instructions exactly. Do not give charcoal without medical direction.

steps.instruction: For INHALED poison: move to fresh air immediately. Open doors and windows. Do NOT enter contaminated area without protection.
steps.tip: If the toxic gas has a smell, hold your breath while moving them. Get upwind.

steps.instruction: If unconscious: place in recovery position. Monitor breathing. If breathing stops, begin CPR but be cautious of chemical exposure during mouth-to-mouth.
steps.tip: Use a barrier device for rescue breaths if possible with chemical poisoning.

do_not: Do NOT induce vomiting without medical advice
Do NOT give food or liquids unless instructed
Do NOT enter an area with toxic fumes without protection
